channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump’s ICE nabs child sex offenders among 530+ illegal immigrants caught in single day
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) arrested a slew of child sex offenders as part of their operations to pick up illegal immigrants
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump orders release of JFK, RFK and MLK assassination records
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered the release of thousands of classified governmental documents about the 1963 assassination of President John F. Kennedy, which has fueled conspiracy theories for decades.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Defense Department pauses all social media posts pending review by incoming secretary
item.description: In the future, all social media posts from the Department of Defense will reflect an emphasis solely on "warfighting and lethality" under President Donald Trump, Fox News has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 11 Dem state AGs criticize 'vague' threats over prosecution for noncompliance with immigration enforcement
item.description: States attorneys general from 11 states have criticized a Justice Department memo warning of possible prosecutions for noncompliance of immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State lawmakers ask education agency for guidance on how Texas schools can respond to Trump’s immigration plans
item.description: More than two dozen Texas Democrats expressed concern about the elimination of a federal policy preventing immigration arrests at schools.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, KPRC 2

item.title: US active duty troops beginning to arrive in Texas and San Diego to support border security
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Active duty military troops will begin arriving in El Paso, Texas, and San Diego on Thursday evening, in what defense officials said is the first batch of the new forces being deployed to secure the southern border.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USDA secretary nominee says politics shouldn't influence California wildfire response
item.description: Efforts by the U.S. Forest Service to fight wildfires in California shouldn't be influenced by politics, President Donald Trump's nominee to head the U.S. Department of Agriculture said Thursday at her Senate confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Murkowski announces she will vote against Hegseth
item.description: Sen. Lisa Murkowski (R-Alaska) announced Thursday afternoon she will vote against Pete Hegseth, President Trump’s nominee to serve as secretary of Defense, citing his past infidelity and allegations of sexual assault and excessive drinking.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship
item.description: U.S. District Judge John C. Coughenour ruled on Thursday in the case brought by the states of Washington, Arizona, Illinois and Oregon. The states argue that the 14th Amendment and Supreme Court case law have cemented birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles if California doesn't change water policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump threatened to withhold federal disaster aid for wildfire-ravaged Los Angeles unless California leaders change the state's approach on its management of water.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: The new Justice Department leadership has put a freeze on civil rights litigation, and suggested it may reconsider police reform agreements negotiated by the Biden administration. That's according to two memos obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump suggests it was a mistake Biden didn't pardon himself
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday repeatedly suggested it was a mistake that former President Biden did not preemptively pardon himself before leaving office. Trump, in a sit-down interview with Fox News host Sean Hannity, also danced around questions on whether Biden should be investigated.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Everything Trump did in the first executive orders and actions of his presidency
item.description: President Donald Trump began his second administration with a blitz of policy actions to reorient U.S. government priorities. Some have an immediate policy impact. Others are more symbolic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Bishop Mariann Budde, former Minnesota bishop, tells NPR ‘I won’t apologize’ for sermon addressing Trump
item.description: The Episcopal bishop of Washington spoke with NPR to discuss her sermon addressing President Trump and why she asked him to have mercy.
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise, MPR News

item.title: Speaker Johnson forms committee to probe Jan. 6, Democrats' original investigation
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Wednesday said he will establish a new select subcommittee that will probe the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot — and likely dig into the now-defunct Jan. 6 select committee that was led by Rep. Bennie Thompson (D-Miss.) and then-Rep. Liz Cheney (R-Wyo.). Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: NJ bill looks to eliminate hurdle for student service members
item.description: FLORHAM PARK, N.J. (PIX11) - From studying to serving: that was National Guard veteran Frank "Doc" Schupp's life in the late 2000s. He was called to deploy to Iraq at the end of his last semester of his junior year at Kean University, in the midst of prepping for finals.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Congress clears GOP-led immigration enforcement bill, with Democrats on board
item.description: The Laken Riley Act would make it easier for federal immigration officials to detain and deport those without legal status who are charged with specific crimes.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo and Deirdre Walsh, MPR News

item.title: Where Donald Trump's executive orders rank historically
item.description: President Donald Trump is on pace to surpass Joe Biden's executive orders signed during his first 100 days in office
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Pritzker, Chicago officials vow to protect immigrant communities
item.description: Chicago's immigrant community and supporters are speaking out, fearful that families could soon be ripped apart.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, NewsNation

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pardoned violent Jan. 6 rioters. Here are 5 of them
item.description: Hundreds of rioters accused of violently assaulting police at the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, were pardoned Monday by President Trump, despite remarks from his allies in recent weeks condemning those defendants.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to probe local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: House Democrats stick to work closer to home as row with Republicans persists at Minnesota Capitol
item.description: Democrats in the Minnesota House are boycotting the Capitol over a disagreement about the power Republicans should hold given a temporary advantage there. They’re doing events in their districts instead.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: First female Coast Guard commandant ousted
item.description: Coast Guard Commandant Adm. Linda Fagan, the first female uniformed leader of an Armed Forces branch in U.S. history, was ousted by the Trump administration with no explanation late Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Florida officials react to Trump inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump was sworn in as the 47th President of the United States on Monday. Here's how Florida officials are responding.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama not attending Donald Trump's inauguration: officials
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The former first lady of the U.S., Michelle Obama, isn't attending President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration on Monday, according to her office. It's not clear why she's not at the swearing-in ceremony. Her husband, former President Barack Obama, is taking part in the event.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11
